etymology.description: phonetic 牙(牙) (yá, "tooth") + semantic 邑(邑) (yì, "town; city") -> originally a place name -> borrowed for "wrong; evil"
etymology.interpretation: The character originally referred to a place name, as indicated by the semantic component 邑(邑) (yì, "town; city"), suggesting a geographic context. The phonetic component 牙(牙) (yá, "tooth") contributes the sound. Over time, it was phonically repurposed for the sense "wrong; evil," which was initially represented by the different character 衺.

examples.chinese: 他的动机看起来不太邪(他的動機看起來不太邪)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de dòngjī kàn qǐlái bù tài yé.
examples.english: His motives do not seem very wrong.

examples.chinese: 如果做坏事是邪的(如果做壞事是邪的)，那么做好事就是对的(那麼做好事就是對的)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ zuò huàishì shì yé de, nàme zuò hǎoshì jiù shì duì de.
examples.english: If doing bad things is wrong, then doing good things is right.
